data.title: MRT.UN: Retail outperformed office in Q1 2026, with stable income and active redevelopment projects
data.description:
Q1 2026 results were stable year-over-year, with retail strength offsetting office softness. Occupancy dipped to 84.8% due to tenant failures, but new leasing and redevelopment initiatives are underway. Liquidity remains solid, and refinancing plans are in place for maturing debt.

data.title: GameStop Is Looking to Buy eBay, Report Says. Both Stocks Are Soaring. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Evie Liu

GameStop stock jumped 9% on Friday in after-hours trading after The Wall Street Journal reported the videogame retailer is preparing to make an offer to acquire the e-commerce company eBay, citing people familiar with the matter. eBay shares rose 12.5% on the news.

GameStop has been building a stake in eBay's shares and could submit an offer later this month, the newspaper reported.

GameStop did not immediately respond to a request from comment.

GamesStop has a market value of around $12 billion, while eBay's is about $46 billion.

GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen told the Journal in late January that he was looking for potential deal targets as part of a plan to scale the company beyond videogames and collectibles.

Michael Burry, the investor known for his bet against the housing market ahead of the subprime mortgage crisis, said in late January that he has been purchasing GameStop shares, since he believes in Cohen's ability to deploy the company's cash, similar to what Warren Buffett did with Berkshire Hathaway.

GameStop had around $9 billion in cash by the end of March, up from $4.8 billion a year earlier. The stock has gained 29% year to date.

eBay's shares are up 36% so far this year, driven by strong growth in selling collectibles, expanding live commerce, and strategic acquisitions such as Depop.

Write to Evie Liu at evie.liu@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AGNICO EAGLE ANNOUNCES ELECTION OF DIRECTORS
data.description:
TORONTO, May 1, 2026 /CNW/ - Agnico Eagle Mines Limited ("Agnico Eagle") today announced that, at the annual and special meeting of shareholders held on May 1, 2026, the eleven candidates listed in the management information circular dated March 19, 2026 were duly elected as directors of Agnico Eagle.

Detailed results of the vote are set out below:

 
Nominee       Votes For    Votes         Total Votes  Percentage    Percentage 
                           Withheld       Cast        of Votes For   of Votes 
                                                                     Withheld 
Leona 
 Aglukkaq     376,559,887     3,993,239  380,553,126       98.95 %      1.05 % 
Ammar 
 Al-Joundi    373,674,892     6,878,232  380,553,124       98.19 %      1.81 % 
Sean Boyd     357,792,066    22,761,060  380,553,126       94.02 %      5.98 % 
Martine A. 
 Celej        370,994,353     9,558,772  380,553,125       97.49 %      2.51 % 
Jonathan 
 Gill         379,925,013       628,112  380,553,125       99.83 %      0.17 % 
Peter 
 Grosskopf    363,585,926    16,967,200  380,553,126       95.54 %      4.46 % 
Elizabeth 
 Lewis-Gray   380,131,083       422,042  380,553,125       99.89 %      0.11 % 
Deborah 
 McCombe      374,077,284     6,475,841  380,553,125       98.30 %      1.70 % 
Jeffrey Parr  371,811,784     8,741,342  380,553,126       97.70 %      2.30 % 
J. Merfyn 
 Roberts      365,892,748    14,660,378  380,553,126       96.15 %      3.85 % 
Jamie C. 
 Sokalsky     368,901,770    11,651,356  380,553,126       96.94 %      3.06 %

Biographical information on all directors is available at www.agnicoeagle.com.

About Agnico Eagle

Canadian-based and led, Agnico Eagle is Canada's largest mining company and the second largest gold producer in the world, operating mines in Canada, Australia, Finland and Mexico. The Company is advancing a pipeline of high-quality development projects in these regions to support sustainable growth over the next decade. Agnico Eagle is a partner of choice within the mining industry, recognized globally for its leading sustainability practices. Agnico Eagle was founded in 1957 and has consistently created value for its shareholders, declaring a cash dividend every year since 1983.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/agnico-eagle-announces-election-of-directors-302760523.html

SOURCE Agnico Eagle Mines Limited

data.title: Miami International, Nathan's Famous, and 3 More Stocks See Action From Activist Investors — Barrons.com
data.description:
These disclosures are from 13Ds filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. 13Ds are filed within 10 days of an entity's attaining more than 5% in any class of a company's securities. Subsequent changes in holdings or intentions must be reported in amended filings. This material is from April 23 through April 29, 2026. Source: VerityData

Increases in Holdings

Miami International Holdings (MIAX) Horizon Kinetics raised its stake in the exchange operator to 11,162,809 shares. Horizon Kinetics did so through the purchase of 100,504 Miami International Holdings shares from April 10 through April 27 at per share prices ranging from $41.37 to $46.52, then slightly offset those purchases through the sale of 233 shares from April 10 through April 21 at $41.34 to $45.18. On April 24, Horizon Managing Director Eric Sites was elected to the board of directors.

Following the latest activity, Horizon Kinetics continues to own a 12.2% stake in Miami International Holdings' outstanding stock. Shares of Miami International Holdings have gained roughly 9.1% in value in 2026.

MeiraGTx Holdings(MGTX) Perceptive Advisors raised its stake in the gene therapy company to 11,742,117 shares. Perceptive Advisors did so through the purchase of 555,555 MeiraGTx shares on April 17 at a price of $9 per share pursuant to the company's underwritten public offering.

the latest purchase, Perceptive Advisors continues to own a 12.6% stake in MeiraGTx's outstanding stock. Shares of MeiraGTx have gained approximately 14.6% in value in 2026.

Monro(MNRO) Gamco Investors raised its stake in the operator of automobile service stations to 3,195,847 shares. Gamco Investors did so through the purchase of 489,085 Monro shares from March 11 through April 24 at per share prices ranging from $14.98 to $18.12, and slightly offset those purchases through the sale of 47,230 shares from March 16 through April 15 at $15.36 to $16.82.

Following the latest activity, Gamco Investors continues to own a 10.7% stake in Monro's outstanding stock. Shares of Monro have lost approximately 15% of their value in 2026.

Decreases in Holdings

Primis Financial (FRST) Castle Creek Capital Partners VII lowered its stake in the bank holding company to 1,076,499 shares. Castle Creek did so through the sale of 254,175 Primis Financial shares from April 13 through April 17 at per share prices ranging from $13.76 to $14.30.

Following the latest sales, Castle Creek Capital Partners continues to own 4.4% of Primis Financial's outstanding stock, placing Castle Creek below the 5% threshold that would require it to report further sales. Shares of Primis Financial have gained roughly 11.8% in value in 2026.

Nathan's Famous(NATH) Gamco Investors lowered its stake in the fast-food franchiser and licenser to 481,086 shares. Gamco Investors did so through the sale of 39,000 Nathan's Famous shares from Feb. 27 through April 22 at per share prices ranging from $100.53 to $101.84, and slightly offset those sales through the purchase of 6,100 shares from March 5 through March 18 at $100.40 to $100.80.

Following the latest activity, Gamco Investors continues to own an 11.8% stake in Nathan's Famous' outstanding stock. Shares of Nathan's Famous have gained approximately 9.1% in value in 2026.

Email: editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Lockheed Martin Gets Space-Based Interceptor Program Contract From Space Force
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Lockheed Martin received a U.S. Space Force contract to work on the Space-Based Interceptor program, a missile defense system built using a constellation of low Earth orbit interceptors.

The defense contractor said it will accelerate the development, testing and integration of SBI capabilities. It didn't disclose the size of the contract.

"We're investing in technology and infrastructure, while bringing together the strength of the full industrial base, to deliver advanced capabilities like SBI faster and are committed to delivering an integrated demonstration by 2028," Lockheed Martin Space President Robert Lightfoot said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Basis steady-firmer; weather in focus
data.description:
Basis bids for hard red winter wheat were steady to stronger in the southern U.S. Plains on Friday, reflecting demand from domestic flour millers while farmers remain reluctant sellers as they await more clarity on how drought has affected prospects for the 2026 harvest, dealers said.

  • Light rain was expected early next week in the central Plains but drought stress is expected to persist in half of the HRW wheat belt, the Commodity Weather Group said in a client note.

  • The showers might arrive too late to offer much benefit to the hardest-hit winter wheat in western Oklahoma. "I don't think the rain is going to save it," one merchandiser said.

  • Meanwhile, the National Weather Service posted frost advisories through Saturday morning from the Texas Panhandle into northwest Kansas and freeze warnings across central Nebraska.

  • The spot basis for wheat improved by 5 cents a bushel at Catoosa, Oklahoma.

  • Protein premiums for railcar HRW wheat to and through Kansas City rose by 15 cents per bushel for "ordinary" wheat with less than 11.0% protein, and by 2 cents for wheat with 11.0% and 11.2% protein. Premiums for other grades were unchanged.

  • K.C. wheat futures ended higher on Friday, hovering near two-year highs set at mid-week. Benchmark K.C. July wheat (KWN26) settled up 1 cent at $6.94-1/2 a bushel after reaching $7.18-1/2 this week, the highest on a continuous chart of the most-active contract (KWv1) since June 2024.

data.title: Could a Warsh Fed Tame Inflation? — Barrons.com
data.description:
To the Editor: A free market of millions of market actors will always price interest rates more efficiently than 12 Federal Open Market Committee members (" Kevin Warsh Is Ready to Fix the Fed. Inflation Is Standing in the Way," Cover Story, April 24). It will be interesting to see whether the Federal Reserve shrinks the balance sheet further and allows price discovery to take hold of the long-duration bonds. Defining inflation as the personal consumption expenditures price index has also allowed malinvestment of capital to flourish. Inflation has more often tended to manifest in asset prices than consumer prices over the past 30 years. Without Congress getting serious about cutting waste and cutting real spending, any attempt to beat inflation will prove useless.

Richard Saler On Barrons.com

CEOs Earn Their Pay

To the Editor: For context on CEO salaries (" The Highest-Paid CEO Is a Furniture Salesman," Up & Down Wall Street, April 23): The top 76 Major League Baseball players each make over $20 million, with Juan Soto (at $62 million) leading the pack (with three home runs and eight RBIs for the hapless Mets). The top 50 National Basketball Association players average over $33 million, with the Warriors' Steph Curry (at $60 million) the highest-paid — on a team that missed the playoffs.

Lawrence Vocke Napoleon, Ohio

Retirement Caveat

To the Editor: The 30-year period for someone retiring in 1929 continued well after World War II on the winning side of a country not ravaged by war (" The Worst Year to Retire Wasn't 1929. The Creator of the 4% Retirement Rule Says It Was 1968," Retirement, April 19). The U.S. rebuilt much of the world then, with little competition at the outset. In fact, it is often stated that American production for WWII is what brought the U.S. out of the Great Depression. It's also when the U.S. dollar became the world's currency, replacing the British pound. These were unique circumstances compared with 1968 and 2000, and very unlikely to repeat again.

Bruce Musler On Barrons.com

Spiraling Debt

To the Editor: Casey Burgat's Other Voices column is too optimistic (" Congress Is Broken. Investors Are Starting to Notice," April 24). Burgat believes that the investor community is a lot more tolerant of the present situation than it will eventually prove to be. The inability to come to grips with a spiraling budget deficit is all too evident. There is simply no motivation to do otherwise. Anyone who has even superficial familiarity with historical precedents knows that a tipping point will occur when the real burden of the debt is simply too high to overlook by even the most nonchalant.

Juan M. Bracete Apollo Beach, Fla.

Send letters to: mail@barrons.com. To be considered for publication, correspondence must bear the writer's name, address, and phone number. Letters are subject to editing.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Unisys Corp expected to post a loss of 31 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Unisys Corp , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Blue Bell Pennsylvania-based company is expected to report a 3.8% decrease in revenue to $415.744 million from $432.1 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on February 25 2026, for the period ended March 31, was for revenue of $415.000 million. (SALES is based on Total Revenue)

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Unisys Corp is for a loss of 31 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Unisys Corp is $6.00, about 125.6% above its last closing price of $2.66

Previous quarterly performance (using preferred earnings measure in US dollars). ​

data.description:

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1.3% in the last three months. ​

data.title: Unitil Corp expected to post earnings of $1.86 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Unitil Corp , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 4 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Hampton New Hampshire-based company is expected to report a 4.9% increase in revenue to $179.13 million from $170.8 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Unitil Corp is for earnings of $1.86 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 2.0% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Unitil Corp is $56.50, about 9% above its last closing price of $51.85
